symptoms.symptom_name: Intermittent claudication — cramping arch/foot pain on walking, relieved by rest, over 6 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic exertional distal ischaemic pain preceding rest pain fits progressive distal occlusive disease.
Claudication is the classic early symptom of PAD too — non-discriminating on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic rest pain waking the patient from sleep

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest pain is a defining criterion of critical limb ischaemia irrespective of cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blackened, cold, necrotic distal second toe (dry gangrene) visible on video

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tissue loss/gangrene is definitional of critical limb ischaemia and mandates urgent vascular assessment.
Distal digital gangrene is the characteristic end-point of Buerger's in a young smoker.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent pedal pulses with preserved femoral and popliteal pulses (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distal occlusion with proximal sparing is characteristic of Buerger's and argues against proximal atherosclerotic PAD.
Classic atherosclerotic PAD tends to involve proximal segments with a corresponding proximal pulse deficit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy tobacco use (~30 cigarettes/day for 15 years)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Buerger's occurs almost exclusively in tobacco users and continued use drives progression — heavy smoking is near-essential to the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent migratory superficial thrombophlebitis — tender red cords on the lower legs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory superficial thrombophlebitis is a recognised associated feature of Buerger's disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Capillary refill greater than 5 seconds in the affected foot (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Markedly prolonged capillary refill objectively corroborates severe distal hypoperfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ineffective heavy self-medication with over-the-counter NSAIDs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe ischaemic rest pain unresponsive to OTC analgesia is consistent with the severity of critical limb ischaemia and signals a need for potent analgesia in hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Corkscrew collateral vessels on conventional peripheral angiography

investigation_details.expected_result: Segmental distal occlusions with characteristic corkscrew-shaped collateral vessels; normal proximal arteries
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific vascular pattern confirming Buerger's is not demonstrated; done in hospital as part of the vascular surgical work-up, not remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Corkscrew collaterals with distal segmental occlusion and normal proximal vessels are highly characteristic of Buerger's disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no spreading cellulitis, no systemic sepsis at presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of sepsis supports DRY gangrene and a limb (T4) rather than immediately life-threatening (T5) emergency now, but must be re-screened as infection can supervene.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Distal digital gangrene + preserved proximal pulses + heavy tobacco use + migratory superficial thrombophlebitis in a young patient is the hallmark constellation of Buerger's disease.
Rest pain plus tissue loss plus absent pedal pulses/prolonged capillary refill define a limb-threatening emergency requiring urgent vascular assessment, independent of the underlying cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person vascular surgical assessment with hands-on pulse examination and handheld Doppler / ankle-brachial pressure index
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms distal arterial occlusion with proximal sparing and quantifies ischaemia — must be done urgently in person; cannot be obtained via telehealth.
Demonstrates the corkscrew collaterals and distal occlusive pattern and maps the anatomy for possible revascularisation — a hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods including inflammatory markers, and screening for atherosclerotic risk (glucose/HbA1c, lipids) and a thrombophilia/autoimmune screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes diabetes and other mimics, and detects secondary infection; part of the hospital work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat home/OTC analgesia trial before escalation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient has already over-used OTC NSAIDs without benefit; further home analgesia in place of escalation delays limb salvage and risks NSAID toxicity.
